summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function: a nightly Timer-triggered Python 3.11 function that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI, validates the roster response, and persists structured JSON to dedicated Azure Blob Storage using Managed Identity throughout. Covers infrastructure provisioning (Bicep), application implementation, security hardening, observability, CI/CD pipeline, and release readiness.
assumptions: TRAPI supports Azure AD Managed Identity bearer token authentication; if not, a Key Vault secret fallback will be used.
TRAPI endpoint base URL and GPT-4o model version will be confirmed by rciapala before APP-01 begins.
Player scope is limited to the 25-man active roster (24–28 validation range) unless rciapala clarifies otherwise.
TRAPI is accessible over the public internet; no VNet integration required for initial deployment.
Consumption Plan is acceptable; cold-start risk at 2 AM UTC is tolerated unless rciapala mandates a strict SLA.
GitHub Actions OIDC federated credential is available for Azure deployments (no long-lived service principal secrets).
Existing infra/ Bicep modules (storage, functionapp, rbac, monitoring, alerts) will be extended, not replaced.
A staging deployment slot is available or will be provisioned on the Consumption Plan (requires Standard or above for slots — may need Premium).
Application Insights workspace already exists or will be provisioned as part of INF-02.
The plan is NOT automatically approved; a human review step is required.
open_questions: Does TRAPI support Azure AD Managed Identity bearer tokens, or is a client secret / API key required?
What is the TRAPI base URL, API path, and GPT-4o model version string to pin?
Should the roster include only the 25-man active roster, or also coaching staff, injured list, and front-office?
Is TRAPI network-restricted (requires VNet/private endpoint) or accessible over the public internet?
Is there a strict SLA on the 2 AM UTC execution window that would require Premium Plan always-ready instances instead of Consumption Plan?
Does the Consumption Plan support deployment slots, or must the hosting plan be upgraded to enable staging slot swap?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Resolve blockers, establish environment, provision all cloud infrastructure, and configure CI/CD pipeline so that application development can begin on a stable, secure foundation.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and open questions triaged
Azure subscription and resource group access confirmed
GitHub repository and Actions permissions confirmed
TRAPI endpoint and auth mechanism confirmed (or Key Vault fallback decision made)
exit_criteria: Task plan reviewed and approved (PRJ-01)
All Bicep infrastructure deployed to dev/staging environment
CI/CD pipeline executes end-to-end (lint, test, deploy to staging)
Managed Identity role assignments verified
Key Vault provisioned and accessible from Function App
TRAPI auth validated with a manual token acquisition test

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement all functional requirements: TRAPI client with prompt template, response schema validation, blob writer with conditional PUT, and nightly Timer Trigger wiring.
entry_criteria: Prompt template drafted and stored in prompts/
Infrastructure deployed and Managed Identity role assignments active
exit_criteria: GetAndStoreYankeesRoster function executes successfully in staging
Roster blob written to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json
Validation rejects out-of-range responses and writes to failed/ prefix
Timer Trigger fires at 2 AM UTC with useMonitor: true
Unit tests passing with ≥80% coverage on core modules

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, security enforcement, observability instrumentation, and data quality alerting to production-harden the solution.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
exit_criteria: Exponential-backoff retry (3 retries, 45s timeout) verified under fault injection
Conditional PUT idempotency verified on same-day retrigger
All three alert rules active in Application Insights
player_count_returned custom metric emitted and visible in App Insights
Structured log events confirmed for all key lifecycle points
Security scan (no hardcoded secrets, least-privilege RBAC) passing
Integration tests passing against staging blob output

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Complete end-to-end staging validation, production deployment via slot swap, documentation, and confirm monitoring is live.
entry_criteria: All acceptance criteria verified in staging
exit_criteria: Production slot swap executed via GitHub Actions
Smoke test passes against production (blob written, alerts active)
README complete with local dev setup, TRAPI auth instructions, and blob naming convention
All success criteria from spec.md checked off
Monitoring and alerting confirmed active in production

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan for completeness, sequencing, and alignment with spec.md. Resolve open questions (especially TRAPI auth and endpoint) before approving. Approve by closing this issue or applying an 'approved' label.
outputs: Resolved open questions documented in repo
acceptance_criteria: All open questions triaged — at minimum TRAPI auth mechanism and endpoint confirmed or deferred with explicit fallback decision
Task sequencing and wave structure reviewed and accepted
Player scope (active roster only vs. broader) confirmed
Consumption vs. Premium Plan decision documented
Visual dependency graph reviewed (see below)
Plan approved by rciapala or designated reviewer
risks: TRAPI auth uncertainty blocks APP-01 and APP-02 — unblocking this is the single highest-priority action
Consumption Plan slot availability may require hosting plan change, impacting INF-03 scope

tasks.title: Provision dedicated storage account and blob container via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/storage.bicep to provision a dedicated Standard_LRS storage account with Cool access tier, private yankees-roster container (no anonymous access), soft-delete enabled with 7-day retention, and allowSharedKeyAccess:false.
acceptance_criteria: Storage account provisioned with kind=StorageV2, sku=Standard_LRS, accessTier=Cool
Container yankees-roster exists with publicAccess:None
Soft-delete enabled with retentionDays=7
allowSharedKeyAccess=false on storage account
Bicep deployment completes without errors in dev resource group
Storage account is separate from the Function App host storage account
risks: Cool access tier may incur early-deletion fees if blobs are deleted within 30 days; confirm retention policy aligns with cost expectations

tasks.title: Provision Key Vault and Application Insights via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend infra/main.bicep to provision an Azure Key Vault (zone-redundant, RBAC authorization model) and confirm Application Insights workspace with 30-day retention and sampling enabled. Wire APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING into Function App settings.
acceptance_criteria: Key Vault provisioned with enableRbacAuthorization=true and zoneRedundant (or equivalent SKU)
Application Insights connected to Log Analytics workspace with retentionInDays=30
Sampling enabled on Application Insights (adaptive sampling or fixed-rate)
APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING wired into Function App app settings
Key Vault soft-delete and purge protection enabled
Bicep deployment completes without errors
risks: Key Vault zone-redundancy depends on region support; verify target region supports ZRS Key Vault SKU

tasks.title: Provision Function App staging slot and configure host.json
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/functionapp.bicep to add a staging deployment slot. Update host.json to set functionTimeout=120s, maxConcurrentCalls=1, and extensionBundle version pinned. Confirm hosting plan supports slots (may require Premium Plan).
acceptance_criteria: Staging slot provisioned and accessible
host.json functionTimeout set to 120 (seconds)
host.json extensions.http.maxConcurrentRequests or timer maxConcurrentCalls=1 configured
Hosting plan confirmed to support slots (upgrade to Premium/Standard if Consumption does not)
Staging slot app settings include APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING and Key Vault references
Bicep deployment completes without errors
risks: Azure Functions Consumption Plan does not support deployment slots — may require upgrade to Premium Plan, increasing cost above $1/month estimate

tasks.title: Provision Managed Identity RBAC role assignments via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/rbac.bicep to assign Storage Blob Data Contributor scoped to the yankees-roster container, Key Vault Secrets User on the Key Vault, and document the TRAPI auth scope for Managed Identity bearer token acquisition.
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor role assigned to Function App Managed Identity scoped to yankees-roster container (not account-level)
Key Vault Secrets User role assigned to Function App Managed Identity on the Key Vault
No account-level or subscription-level over-privileged assignments
Role assignments verified via az role assignment list in dev environment
TRAPI auth scope documented (or Key Vault secret placeholder created if MI not supported)
risks: Container-scoped RBAC requires the container to exist before assignment — depends on INF-01 completion
TRAPI auth scope unknown — if MI not supported, a Key Vault secret must be seeded manually before APP-01

tasks.title: Configure Key Vault references in Function App settings
tasks.description: Replace any plaintext sensitive app settings (TRAPI endpoint, auth scope, model version) with Key Vault references (@Microsoft.KeyVault(SecretUri=...)) in the Function App configuration. Seed initial secret values into Key Vault.
inputs: INF-04 output (Key Vault Secrets User assignment)
TRAPI endpoint and auth details from rciapala
outputs: infra/modules/functionapp.bicep (updated app settings with KV references)
Key Vault secrets seeded (TRAPI-ENDPOINT, TRAPI-AUTH-SCOPE, TRAPI-MODEL-VERSION)
acceptance_criteria: No plaintext secrets in Function App app settings or source code
TRAPI_ENDPOINT, TRAPI_AUTH_SCOPE, TRAPI_MODEL_VERSION app settings resolve via Key Vault references
Function App can read Key Vault secrets at startup (verify via az functionapp config appsettings list)
Key Vault access logs show successful secret reads by Function App Managed Identity
Secret rotation procedure documented
risks: TRAPI auth mechanism unconfirmed — if not MI-based, a fallback API key must be stored in Key Vault before this task can be completed

tasks.title: Implement GitHub Actions OIDC federated credential for Azure deployments
tasks.description: Configure GitHub Actions to use OIDC federated identity (no long-lived service principal secrets) for authenticating to Azure during infrastructure and function deployments. Add the federated credential to the deployment service principal in Bicep or portal.
inputs: Azure AD app registration or User-assigned Managed Identity for GH Actions
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow authenticates to Azure via OIDC (no AZURE_CREDENTIALS secret with client secret)
Deployment workflow successfully deploys Bicep and function app code using federated identity
No long-lived credentials stored in GitHub secrets
Least-privilege role assigned to GH Actions identity (Contributor scoped to resource group)
risks: If the repo uses an existing service principal with client secrets, migration to OIDC requires coordination with repo admins

tasks.title: Build CI/CD pipeline with staging slot deploy and swap
tasks.description: Create or update .github/workflows/cd.yml to: lint and test on PR, deploy to staging slot on push to main, run smoke test against staging, then swap staging to production. Gate swap on smoke test pass.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow runs pytest and linter on every PR targeting main
CD workflow deploys to staging slot on push to main
Smoke test runs against staging slot after deploy
Slot swap to production only occurs if smoke test passes
Failed smoke test leaves staging slot deployed and production untouched
Workflow uses OIDC federated identity (no plaintext secrets)
risks: Slot swap requires Premium Plan or Standard Plan — Consumption Plan limitation may block this task until INF-03 hosting plan decision is resolved

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client with Managed Identity auth and exponential-backoff retry
tasks.description: Implement or extend app/trapi_client.py to acquire a Managed Identity bearer token for the TRAPI auth scope, POST the GPT-4o prompt to the pinned model version endpoint, enforce a 45-second HTTP timeout, and retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff on transient failures.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint and auth scope (from SEC-01 / Key Vault)
acceptance_criteria: Managed Identity bearer token acquired via DefaultAzureCredential with correct TRAPI scope
HTTP POST to TRAPI endpoint uses pinned GPT-4o model version from app settings
Request timeout set to 45 seconds
3 retries with exponential backoff on HTTP 429, 500, 502, 503, 504 responses
Non-retryable errors (4xx except 429) raise immediately without retry
Prompt loaded from prompts/get_1985_yankees.txt at runtime (not hardcoded)
Unit tests mock TRAPI HTTP calls and verify retry and timeout behavior
risks: TRAPI auth scope must be confirmed before this task; blocked if open question 1 is unresolved
Pinned model version string must be confirmed with TRAPI team to avoid 404 on model lookup

tasks.title: Implement response schema validation and failed-blob routing
tasks.description: Implement app/validator.py to validate the TRAPI response against the expected JSON schema (players array with name/position/jersey_number) and player count range (24–28). On validation failure, route the raw response to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json and raise a custom AppInsights event.
acceptance_criteria: Schema validation rejects responses missing players array or with wrong field types
Player count outside 24–28 range triggers validation failure path
Failed responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json in dedicated storage account
Validation failure emits a structured log event and custom AppInsights metric
Valid responses pass through to blob write path without modification
Unit tests cover: valid response, schema violation, under-count (< 24), over-count (> 28)
risks: Player count range (24–28) may need adjustment if open question 3 (player scope) is resolved to include broader roster
GPT-4o output is non-deterministic; schema may vary across model versions — pin model version in APP-01 to reduce variance

tasks.title: Implement blob writer with conditional PUT and Managed Identity auth
tasks.description: Implement or extend app/blob_writer.py to write validated roster JSON to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json using BlobClient with DefaultAzureCredential and a conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) to prevent duplicate writes on same-day retrigger.
inputs: INF-04 output (Storage Blob Data Contributor assignment)
outputs: app/blob_writer.py (updated with conditional PUT and MI auth)
acceptance_criteria: Blob written to dedicated storage account (not host storage) using DefaultAzureCredential
Blob name follows yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json convention with UTC date
Conditional PUT (If-None-Match: *) prevents overwrite on same-day retrigger (returns 409 and logs, does not raise)
Failed responses written to yankees-roster/failed/{run_date_utc}.json path
No storage account connection strings or keys in code or environment variables
Unit tests cover: successful write, duplicate write (409 handled), failed-path write
risks: Container-scoped RBAC must be active before integration testing — depends on INF-04
Cool access tier has a 30-day minimum storage duration; deleting blobs before 30 days incurs early deletion charges

tasks.title: Implement Timer Trigger function wiring and structured logging
tasks.description: Implement app/function_app.py with the GetAndStoreYankeesRoster Timer Trigger (schedule 0 0 2 * * *, useMonitor=true, UTC), orchestrating TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer. Emit all structured log events (function_started, trapi_request_sent, trapi_response_received, blob_write_succeeded, function_completed) and the player_count_returned custom metric via OpenTelemetry SDK.
outputs: requirements-prod.txt (updated with opentelemetry deps if needed)
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger schedule is '0 0 2 * * *' with run_on_startup=False
useMonitor=True set on the Timer Trigger binding
All five structured log events emitted with required fields (model version, prompt hash, token count, latency ms, player count, blob URI)
player_count_returned custom metric emitted to Application Insights on each successful run
Function completes in < 60 seconds under normal TRAPI latency
functionTimeout in host.json set to 120 seconds
maxConcurrentCalls=1 configured to prevent parallel executions
risks: OpenTelemetry SDK integration with Azure Functions v2 Python may require additional configuration; test early
Cold-start on Consumption Plan may add 5–15 seconds to execution time

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights alert rules via Bicep
tasks.description: Extend infra/modules/alerts.bicep to add three alert rules: (1) execution failure count > 0 in 1-hour window, (2) function duration > 90 seconds, (3) player_count_returned custom metric < 24 or > 40. All alerts notify rciapala via email Action Group.
acceptance_criteria: Execution failure alert fires within 5 minutes of a function failure in staging test
Duration alert threshold set to 90 seconds (PT90S evaluation window)
Data quality alert fires when player_count_returned metric is outside [24, 40]
Email Action Group sends to rciapala email address
Alert rules deployed via Bicep (not manually configured)
All three alerts verified in staging by triggering synthetic failures
risks: Custom metric player_count_returned must be emitted by APP-04 before the data quality alert can be tested
Scheduled Query Rule (log-based) alerts have a minimum 5-minute evaluation frequency on Consumption

tasks.title: Validate Timer Trigger monitor and missed-execution surfacing
tasks.description: Verify that useMonitor=True on the Timer Trigger correctly surfaces missed executions in Application Insights. Document the mechanism and test by simulating a missed trigger in the staging environment.
outputs: Test evidence of missed-execution detection in App Insights
acceptance_criteria: useMonitor=True confirmed in function binding configuration
Missed execution surfaces as an alert or log entry in Application Insights (test in staging)
Alert or log entry is actionable (includes run_date, function name, next scheduled time)
Behavior documented in README operations section
risks: Timer monitor behavior on Consumption Plan may differ from Premium Plan due to scale-to-zero; validate in actual staging environment

tasks.title: Implement and verify idempotency under same-day retrigger
tasks.description: Verify end-to-end idempotency by manually retriggering the function twice on the same UTC day in staging. Confirm that the second run detects the existing blob via conditional PUT (409), logs appropriately, and does not overwrite or error fatally.
outputs: Test evidence of idempotent behavior (logs + blob state)
acceptance_criteria: First run on a given UTC date writes blob successfully
Second run on the same UTC date detects existing blob (409) and exits cleanly without error
No duplicate blob or overwrite occurs
Log event emitted on duplicate detection (idempotent skip logged with blob URI)
Function execution status reported as success (not failure) on duplicate-skip run
risks: If conditional PUT raises an unhandled exception instead of logging and continuing, function will report failure and trigger the failure alert — ensure APP-03 handles 409 gracefully

tasks.title: Write unit tests for TRAPI client, validator, and blob writer
tasks.description: Write pytest unit tests in tests/ covering: TRAPI client retry/timeout behavior (mocked HTTP), response schema validation (valid, schema violation, count out of range), blob writer conditional PUT (success and 409 handling), and structured log event emission.
acceptance_criteria: pytest suite passes with 0 failures
≥80% line coverage on app/trapi_client.py, app/validator.py, app/blob_writer.py
TRAPI retry test verifies 3 retries with exponential backoff under mocked 503 responses
Validator test asserts Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, Rickey Henderson appear in a valid response
Blob writer test verifies conditional PUT header (If-None-Match: *) is sent
All tests runnable with: python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=app --cov-report=term-missing
risks: Tests must mock azure-identity and azure-storage-blob SDK calls to avoid live Azure dependencies in CI

tasks.title: Write integration smoke test for staging slot validation
tasks.description: Write a smoke test script (tests/smoke_test.py or shell script) that manually triggers the staging function, waits for completion, and asserts that a blob was written to yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json containing valid JSON with Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test triggers function in staging and waits up to 120 seconds for completion
Asserts blob exists at yankees-roster/{today_utc}.json in dedicated storage account
Asserts blob JSON contains players array with Don Mattingly, Dave Winfield, and Rickey Henderson
Smoke test exits non-zero on assertion failure (blocks CD slot swap)
Smoke test runs within 3 minutes total (compatible with CD pipeline timeout)
risks: Smoke test requires live TRAPI access from GitHub Actions runner — TRAPI network restrictions may block this; confirm with open question 4

tasks.title: End-to-end staging validation against nightly schedule
tasks.description: Let the Timer Trigger fire naturally at 2 AM UTC in the staging environment at least once. Verify the full execution path: TRAPI call, validation, blob write, structured logs, and custom metric all appear in Application Insights as expected.
outputs: Test evidence (App Insights screenshots / log export)
acceptance_criteria: Timer fires at 2:00 AM UTC ± 2 minutes in staging
Blob written to yankees-roster/{run_date_utc}.json with valid roster JSON
All five structured log events appear in Application Insights logs
player_count_returned custom metric emitted and visible in App Insights metrics
No failure alerts fired during successful run
M3 milestone signed off by rciapala
risks: Requires overnight wait for Timer Trigger; plan accordingly to avoid blocking the release wave
Cold-start on Consumption Plan could cause first execution to exceed 60-second performance target

tasks.title: Write README with local dev setup, TRAPI auth, and blob naming convention
tasks.description: Update README.md to include: local development setup (virtual env, local.settings.json, Azurite for local blob), TRAPI authentication instructions (how to obtain MI token or Key Vault secret for local dev), blob naming convention, operations runbook (missed execution, idempotency, alert response), and link to spec.md.
acceptance_criteria: README includes step-by-step local dev setup runnable by a new team member
TRAPI auth instructions cover both MI (prod) and local dev (service principal or interactive auth)
Blob naming convention documented with example (yankees-roster/2026-03-31.json)
Operations section covers: how to check for missed executions, how to respond to failure alerts, idempotency behavior
README reviewed and approved by rciapala
risks: Documentation may become stale if TRAPI auth mechanism changes after spec is finalized
